scope: The use of foundation models like CLIP as feature extractors for multimodal data.
key_concepts: Models like CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training) represent the pinnacle of representations learned by machine (*representasi yang dipelajari mesin*) for multimodal tasks.
CLIP was pretrained on hundreds of millions of image-text pairs to align visual and textual concepts in a shared space.
Instead of engineering features or training joint embeddings from scratch, practitioners can use frozen pretrained multimodal models as off-the-shelf feature extractors.
These models enable zero-shot classification: classifying an image by comparing its embedding to the embeddings of text prompts like 'a photo of a cat', without needing task-specific training data.

definitions.definition: Model *deep learning* berskala besar yang telah dilatih pada himpunan data multimodal raksasa untuk menangkap hubungan semantik universal antar modalitas.
Kemampuan model untuk mengklasifikasikan data ke dalam kategori baru tanpa pernah melihat contoh kategori tersebut secara eksplisit selama pelatihan.

examples: Passing a new dataset of product images into a frozen CLIP image encoder to extract 512-dimensional vectors, which are then used as features in a simple logistic regression model for a completely new task.
exclusions: Architecture details of the Transformer blocks inside CLIP.
